sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Headaches
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a history of headaches for years, initially linked to menstruation, but now occurring more frequently and with greater severity. He notes that exercise exacerbates the headaches and describes them as pulsing on the right side, behind the eye. He has also experienced ringing in his ears and occasional blurred vision, particularly on the side of his vision. He is a new runner, using Asics and New Balance shoes, and has been trying to lose weight through exercise. He has noticed papilledema during the exam.
Findings
Examination reveals papilledema on funduscopic exam. Neurological exam is intact. Gait is normal. Heart sounds are normal. Blood pressure is well controlled.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms are concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension. The presence of papilledema supports this diagnosis.
Plan
I am ordering an MRI of the brain to further evaluate the patient's condition. I will also refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam. We will monitor the patient's symptoms and consider medication to manage the increased intracranial pressure if the MRI confirms the diagnosis.
